sourceMovie.title: On the Waterfront
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: On the Waterfront works as a crime movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wolf of Wall Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If On the Waterfront clicked for you, The Wolf of Wall Street (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: American Hustle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If On the Waterfront clicked for you, American Hustle (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after On the Waterfront?
faq.answer: Try The Wolf of Wall Street, American Hustle, L.A. Confidential—each shares DNA with On the Waterfront while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is On the Waterfront?
faq.answer: On the Waterfront (1954) is best described as Crime, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is On the Waterfront worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
